question.text: A commodity has a spot price of Rs 10,000. The annual interest rate is 8% and holding period is 6 months. Using simple cost-of-carry (no storage), what is the fair futures price?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** F = S + C = 10,000 + (10,000 × 0.08 × 6/12) = 10,000 + 400 = Rs 10,400. **The Trap:** Rs 10,800 uses full year interest. Rs 10,000 ignores the cost of carry. Rs 10,200 uses 3 months instead of 6. **Cross-Exam Context:** Basic cost-of-carry is tested across all derivative exams (I, IV, VIII, XVI). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The cost-of-carry is the minimum return a spot holder should earn — if the futures premium is less than the carry cost, buying spot and selling futures creates an arbitrage opportunity.
